title: The Twenty-third Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
Sermon Date: October 27, 2024

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

The Twenty-third Sunday after Pentecost falls within Ordinary Time in the Christian calendar, specifically in the latter portion of the liturgical year before Advent begins on December 1. Under the Revised Common Lectionary, the readings for this Sunday would likely include Deuteronomy 34:1-12 (or Job 42:1-6) and Luke 18:9-14, featuring Jesus's parable of the Pharisee and the tax collector—a passage about humility and justified sinners. The season emphasizes Christian living and discipleship in the extended period between Pentecost and Advent.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

No major denominational General Convention actions were occurring at this specific time, as the triennial General Convention had concluded in 2024. The Diocese of Colorado would have been in its ordinary operational cycle, with Bishop Konde serving the diocese. Saint John's Cathedral itself, as the seat of the bishop, was likely engaged in routine cathedral programming and the preparation for the coming Advent and Christmas seasons.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Colorado was in the final weeks before the November 5, 2024 election cycle, with significant political activity surrounding ballot measures and races. Denver was experiencing typical late October weather—mild autumn conditions with possible overnight frosts. The Capitol Hill neighborhood, where the Cathedral is located, was in its regular urban rhythm, with Halloween festivities having just passed on October 31 (four days after this sermon).

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The 2024 presidential election was in its final ten days, with Vice President Kamala Harris and former President Donald Trump in the closing phase of campaigning. Hurricane Milton and its aftermath were still being covered in the news cycle, having impacted Florida and other southeastern states in early October. Early voting was underway across the country, with major focus on swing states and turnout predictions dominating national media.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The Israel-Gaza conflict continued with active military operations and significant humanitarian concerns, now more than a year into the war following October 7, 2023. The Russia-Ukraine war remained an ongoing international crisis with winter approaching and questions about sustained military support and diplomatic resolution. Political instability in Venezuela following disputed election results in July continued to generate international attention regarding democratic governance.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture was in the final stretch of election season anxiety and fatigue, with heightened polarization and tension dominating social discourse and news consumption. The MLB World Series and NFL season were in full swing, with Colorado sports fans potentially divided between local Rockies disappointment and national football interest. The broader cultural mood reflected election-year uncertainty, with churches and religious communities often serving as spaces of refuge and reflection amid national divisiveness.
